title: Publish docs as VitePress site on GitHub Pages
summary: Addresses two external review comments from greptile-apps[bot]: (1) replaced global Vue delimiter override in config.ts with targeted ::: v-pre blocks in the two reference docs that contain ${{ }} expressions, preserving standard Vue interpolation site-wide; (2) added a 4-line inline comment to deploy-docs.yaml explaining why the concurrency group intentionally deviates from the repo's standard pattern.

tests_checklist.item: npm run docs:build passes without errors
VitePress renders ${{ }} expressions correctly in reference docs

code_quality_checklist.item: Changes are minimal and narrowly scoped to review feedback
No dead code or commented-out code

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (per-file v-pre vs global config override)
v-pre blocks applied only to files containing ${{ }} expressions (2 of 6 reference docs)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Build errors detected at compile time

defensive_checklist.item: All reference docs with ${{ }} are wrapped (verified via grep)
